profile.education: Yale University (BA, History); Harvard Law School (JD)
profile.background: Navy JAG officer who served in Iraq and at Guantanamo Bay; U.S. Representative (2013-2018); became 46th governor in 2019; ran for president in 2024.

claims.text: Signed SB 300, the Heartbeat Protection Act, prohibiting abortion after detection of fetal cardiac activity (approximately 6 weeks). Became Chapter 2023-21 of Florida law on April 14, 2023.
Florida's FY 2021–2023 budgets excluded state funding for elective abortion providers; DeSantis-era administration prioritized alternatives-to-abortion programs over reproductive-health clinics.

Signed CS/CS/HB 1557, 'Parental Rights in Education,' restricting classroom instruction on sexual orientation and gender identity in K–3 (and requiring age-appropriate limits thereafter). Became Chapter 2022-22 on March 28, 2022; effective July 1, 2022.

Signed CS/HB 543, 'Public Safety,' permitting unlicensed concealed carry of firearms for law-abiding citizens (permitless / 'constitutional carry'). Approved April 3, 2023; Chapter 2023-18; effective July 1, 2023.

Signed SB 1718 (2023), strengthening illegal-immigration enforcement: expanded E-Verify mandate for employers of 25+, criminalized transportation of illegal aliens into Florida, and invalidated out-of-state driver's licenses issued to undocumented immigrants.

Signed HB 1 (2023), making Florida the first universal-school-choice state: Empowerment Scholarship Accounts (ESAs) eligibility opened to all K-12 students regardless of public-school enrollment or family income. Approved March 27, 2023.

Signed HB 7 (2022), the 'Individual Freedom' / 'Stop WOKE' Act, restricting instruction that compels students or employees to endorse specific race/sex-based concepts. Became Chapter 2022-72 on April 22, 2022.

Signed SB 90 (2021) tightening Florida's election-integrity regime: constraints on mail-ballot drop boxes, stricter voter-ID requirements at ballot-request stage, and limits on third-party ballot collection.

candidates.name: Jay Collins
candidates.notes: Former Green Beret, retired Special Forces. Served in U.S. Army Special Operations Command. Lost leg in combat. Florida State Senate District 14 (2022-2024). Sworn in as Lt. Governor February 2025 after Jeanette Nuñez resigned to lead FIU. Strong conservative on border security, veterans issues, and Second Amendment. NRA A rating as state senator.

candidates.name: James Uthmeier
candidates.notes: Sworn in as Florida Attorney General February 17, 2025 after Ashley Moody was appointed to the U.S. Senate. Former DeSantis Chief of Staff. Led the Martha's Vineyard migrant relocation initiative. Filed suit challenging federal immigration policies. Strong on election integrity, parental rights, and opposing ESG/DEI mandates.

candidates.name: Blaise Ingoglia
candidates.notes: Florida Chief Financial Officer since August 2025, appointed by Gov. DeSantis after Jimmy Patronis resigned to run for U.S. House FL-1. Former Florida State Senator (2022-2025) and State Representative (2014-2022). Former FL Republican Party Chairman (2015-2019). Strong fiscal conservative, opposed ESG investment mandates, supported parental rights in education. Heritage Action 95%+ lifetime as state legislator.

candidates.name: Wilton Simpson
candidates.notes: Florida Commissioner of Agriculture since 2023. Former Florida Senate President (2020-2022). Former Florida State Senator (2012-2022). Signed constitutional carry (permitless concealed carry) into law as Senate President. Strong on agricultural policy, water quality, and rural economic development. NRA A+ rating. Supported parental rights in education.

candidates.name: Rick Scott
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 90-95% lifetime. Planned Parenthood Action 0%. ACLU 0%. NRA A+. Led the Senate Republican Conference effort to block Biden executive overreach. Consistently voted YES on confirming Trump 2025 cabinet nominees (Hegseth, Gabbard, Vance's VP confirmation). Co-sponsored Laken Riley Act. Filed the "12% Plan" fiscal framework. Strong on Cuba sanctions, Israel support, and border security.

profile.education: University of Missouri-Kansas City (BS Business Administration), Southern Methodist University (JD)
profile.background: Former Governor of Florida and CEO of Columbia/HCA, the nation's largest hospital company. Navy veteran. Entrepreneur and healthcare executive. In Senate since 2019.

district_offices.address: 3299 Tamiami Trail East, Suite 106, Building F

candidates.name: Ashley Moody
candidates.notes: Appointed to U.S. Senate January 2025 by Gov. DeSantis to fill Marco Rubio's seat after Rubio's confirmation as Secretary of State. Former Florida Attorney General (2019-2025), former Circuit Court Judge. As AG: led legal challenges to Biden immigration policy, joined multi-state suits against federal vaccine mandates, defended FL Parental Rights in Education Act. In Senate: voted YES on Hegseth, Gabbard, Bondi, and other Trump 2025 cabinet nominations. NRA A rating.

profile.education: University of Florida (BA, MA Accounting), University of Florida School of Law (JD), Stetson University (LLM International Law)
profile.background: Former Florida Attorney General and Hillsborough County circuit judge. Former federal prosecutor and Holland & Knight attorney. Appointed to fill Marco Rubio's Senate seat in 2025.

candidates.name: Jimmy Patronis
candidates.notes: Elected to U.S. House FL-1 April 2025 special election to fill Matt Gaetz's seat after Gaetz resigned. Former Florida CFO (2017-2025). Strong fiscal conservative, pro-business. Heritage Action 95%+ lifetime as CFO. NRA A+.

candidates.name: Neal Dunn
candidates.notes: Physician (urologist) before Congress. Heritage Action 85-95% lifetime. NRA A+. Planned Parenthood 0%. ACLU 0%. Strong on veterans healthcare, pro-life, rural healthcare access.

candidates.name: Kat Cammack
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 90%+ lifetime. NRA A+. Planned Parenthood 0%. ACLU 0%. Strong on border security, Second Amendment, agricultural policy. Freedom Caucus member. Co-sponsored SAVE Act requiring proof of citizenship to register to vote.

candidates.name: Aaron Bean
candidates.notes: Former Florida Senate President pro tempore. Heritage Action 90%+. NRA A+. Planned Parenthood 0%. Strong on parental rights, school choice, fiscal restraint. Chair of Subcommittee on Early Childhood, Elementary, and Secondary Education.

candidates.name: John Rutherford
candidates.notes: Former Jacksonville Sheriff. Heritage Action 85-95%. NRA A. Strong on law enforcement, border security. Appropriations Committee member.

candidates.name: Randy Fine
candidates.notes: Elected to U.S. House FL-6 April 2025 special election (replacing Michael Waltz who became National Security Advisor). Former Florida State Representative (2016-2024) and State Senator (2024-2025). Heritage Action 95%+ as state legislator. NRA A+. Strong on Israel, anti-antisemitism legislation, parental rights, school choice, and border security. Jewish Republican.

candidates.name: Cory Mills
candidates.notes: Combat veteran (Army), defense contractor. Heritage Action 90%+. NRA A+. Freedom Caucus member. Strong on border security, Second Amendment, Israel support. Traveled to conflict zones to document hostage situations.

candidates.name: Mike Haridopolos
candidates.notes: Elected to U.S. House FL-8 April 2025 special election (replacing retired Bill Posey). Former Florida Senate President (2010-2012) and State Senator (2000-2012). Heritage Action 85-95% lifetime as state legislator. NRA A+. Strong on fiscal conservatism, defense (Space Coast interests), and pro-life legislation. Represents Brevard County (including Melbourne, Palm Bay, Titusville, Cape Canaveral) plus eastern Orange and Indian River counties in US House FL-8. Deep Space Coast ties — strong advocate for Kennedy Space Center, Cape Canaveral Space Force Base, and commercial space industry.

district_offices.address: 2725 Judge Fran Jamieson Way, Building C

candidates.name: Darren Soto
candidates.notes: Democrat representing a Hispanic-heavy Orlando district. Planned Parenthood 100%. ACLU 90%+. Heritage Action 5-15%. NRA F. Energy and Commerce Committee. Generally liberal but occasionally votes with Republicans on business/tech issues.

candidates.name: Maxwell Frost
candidates.notes: First Gen-Z member of Congress (elected 2022 at age 25). Progressive Democrat, former gun control activist (March for Our Lives organizer). Planned Parenthood 100%. ACLU 100%. Heritage Action 0%. NRA F. Democratic Socialists of America endorsed.

candidates.name: Daniel Webster
candidates.notes: Former Florida Speaker of the House (the first Republican speaker in 122 years). Heritage Action 85-95%. NRA A+. Strong on pro-life, fiscal restraint. Freedom Caucus.

candidates.name: Gus Bilirakis
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 80-90%. NRA A. Strong on veterans, Greek-American interests, healthcare. Energy and Commerce Committee.

candidates.name: Anna Paulina Luna
candidates.notes: Air Force veteran, Mexican-American. Heritage Action 90%+. NRA A+. Freedom Caucus. Strong on border security, election integrity. Led effort to force floor votes on various bills via discharge petition.

candidates.name: Kathy Castor
candidates.notes: Progressive Democrat, chair of House Select Committee on Climate Crisis (2019-2023). Planned Parenthood 100%. ACLU 95%+. Heritage Action 0-5%. NRA F. Strong on climate, LGBTQ rights, abortion access.

candidates.name: Laurel Lee
candidates.notes: Former Florida Secretary of State. Heritage Action 85-95%. NRA A. Strong on election integrity, parental rights. Judiciary Committee member.

candidates.name: Vern Buchanan
candidates.notes: Ranking member of House Ways and Means Subcommittee on Health. Heritage Action 80-90%. NRA A. Planned Parenthood 0%. Pro-life, pro-business, hawkish on foreign policy. Vice Chair of House Ways and Means Committee.

candidates.name: Greg Steube
candidates.notes: Army veteran. Heritage Action 90%+. NRA A+. Judiciary and Veterans Affairs Committees. Strong on Second Amendment, pro-life, judicial reform.

candidates.name: Scott Franklin
candidates.notes: Retired Navy officer. Heritage Action 85-95%. NRA A+. Financial Services Committee. Strong on defense, veterans, fiscal conservatism.

candidates.name: Byron Donalds
candidates.notes: Running for Florida Governor in 2026 (DeSantis term-limited). Heritage Action 90%+. NRA A+. Freedom Caucus. Strong on fiscal conservatism, school choice, border security. Endorsed by Trump for governor.

candidates.name: Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick
candidates.notes: First Haitian-American Democrat in Congress. Planned Parenthood 100%. ACLU 95%+. Heritage Action 0-5%. NRA F. Progressive Democrat.

candidates.name: Brian Mast
candidates.notes: Army combat veteran (lost both legs in Afghanistan). Chair of House Foreign Affairs Committee in the 119th Congress. Heritage Action 85-95%. NRA A+. Strong pro-Israel, hawkish on Iran and China, veterans advocate.

candidates.name: Lois Frankel
candidates.notes: Progressive Democrat, former Mayor of West Palm Beach. Planned Parenthood 100%. ACLU 95%+. Heritage Action 0-5%. NRA F. Co-chair of bipartisan Women's Caucus.

candidates.name: Jared Moskowitz
candidates.notes: Moderate Democrat. Former Florida Division of Emergency Management Director under DeSantis. Voted YES on Laken Riley Act (Jan 2025). Planned Parenthood 100%. ACLU 85-95%. Heritage Action 10-20% (higher than most Ds). NRA F. Oversight Committee member.

candidates.name: Frederica Wilson
candidates.notes: Progressive Democrat, known for signature hats. Planned Parenthood 100%. ACLU 95%+. Heritage Action 0-5%. NRA F.

district_offices.address: 1965 South State Road 7, West Park City Hall
1700 Convention Center Drive, First Floor Suite
1600 NE 126th Street, Scott Galvin Community Center

candidates.name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
candidates.notes: Former DNC Chair (2011-2016). Planned Parenthood 100%. ACLU 95%+. Heritage Action 0-5%. NRA F. Progressive Democrat. Appropriations Committee.

candidates.name: Mario Diaz-Balart
candidates.notes: Cuban-American, strong anti-communist. Heritage Action 75-85%. NRA A. Appropriations Committee. Hard line on Cuba, Venezuela, Nicaragua. Pro-Israel. Pro-life.

candidates.name: Maria Elvira Salazar
candidates.notes: Cuban-American journalist-turned-legislator. Heritage Action 75-85%. NRA A. Strong anti-communist (Cuba, Venezuela). More moderate than some FL Republicans on immigration reform (has supported pathway-to-citizenship discussions for Dreamers). Pro-life. Strong pro-Israel.

candidates.name: Carlos A. Gimenez
candidates.notes: Cuban-American, former Miami-Dade County Mayor (2011-2020). Heritage Action 80-90%. NRA A. Hard line on Cuba, Venezuela, China. Strong on border security, Israel. Pro-life.

candidates.name: Debbie Mayfield
candidates.notes: Florida State Senator, District 19 (covers most of Brevard County including Melbourne, Palm Bay, Titusville, and the Space Coast). Returned to Senate January 2025 after winning special election. Previously served FL Senate 2016-2024 and FL House 2008-2016. Heritage Action 95%+ lifetime. NRA A+. Planned Parenthood 0%. Strong on parental rights (authored Parental Rights in Education Act companion bill), pro-life, and Second Amendment. Chair of Senate Rules Committee during previous tenure.

candidates.name: Ben Albritton
candidates.notes: Elected Florida Senate President for the 2024–2026 term. Republican from central Florida (FL Senate District 27, covering Polk, Hardee, Highlands, and DeSoto counties plus parts of Okeechobee and Osceola). Former Florida House member. Strong conservative on agricultural policy, fiscal restraint, and Christian heritage themes. Signed onto priority legislation on property tax relief, hurricane recovery, and education accountability during his presidency. President of the Florida Senate — verified via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_the_Florida_Senate.

candidates.name: Webster Barnaby
candidates.notes: Florida State Representative, District 29 (Volusia County, borders northern Brevard). Republican. Pastor and conservative Christian leader. Heritage Action 95%+. NRA A+. Filed Florida legislation targeting "drag shows in front of children" and other socially conservative measures. Known for faith-forward political speech.

candidates.name: Tyler I. Sirois
candidates.notes: Florida State Representative, District 31 (north Brevard — Titusville, Cocoa, Cape Canaveral, Merritt Island). Elected 2018, in office since 2019. Republican. Former Brevard County Assistant State Attorney. Heritage Action 90%+. NRA A+. Planned Parenthood 0%. Strong on pro-life legislation, Second Amendment, parental rights, space industry support (KSC). Chair of State House Criminal Justice Subcommittee in prior terms.

candidates.name: Brian Hodgers
candidates.notes: Florida State Representative, District 32 (central Brevard — Melbourne area, including much of the city of Melbourne). Elected 2024. Republican. Melbourne-area real estate broker. Campaigned on property tax relief, parental rights, Second Amendment protection, and fiscal restraint. Endorsed by Americans for Prosperity, Florida Right to Life. NRA A+.

candidates.name: Monique Miller
candidates.notes: Florida State Representative, District 33 (south Brevard — Palm Bay area, south Melbourne, Melbourne Beach). Elected 2024. Republican. Business owner and conservative activist. Campaigned on parental rights, election integrity, and border security. Aligned with the Florida Freedom Caucus / America First wing. NRA A+.

profile.education: University of Missouri-Kansas City (BS Business Administration), Southern Methodist University (JD)
profile.background: Former Governor of Florida and CEO of Columbia/HCA, the nation's largest hospital company. Navy veteran. Entrepreneur and healthcare executive. In Senate since 2019.

candidates.name: Daniel Perez
candidates.notes: Elected Speaker of the Florida House of Representatives for the 2024–2026 term. Republican representing FL House District 116 (Miami-Dade County). Cuban-American. Strong conservative on immigration enforcement, anti-communism (Cuba/Venezuela), parental rights, and fiscal policy. Has pushed education reform, insurance regulation, and condo safety legislation during his speakership.

candidates.name: Eileen Higgins
candidates.notes: First woman mayor of Miami. First Democrat elected mayor since 1997. Former Miami-Dade County Commissioner (2018-2025). Sworn in Dec 2025 after runoff victory over Emilio Gonzalez. Has vowed to limit cooperation with ICE. Elected Miami Mayor November 2024 to fill seat vacated by Francis Suarez. Previously served as Miami-Dade County Commissioner (District 5) from 2018-2024. Progressive Democrat focused on affordable housing, transit expansion, and climate resilience. Planned Parenthood endorsed. First Latina Mayor of Miami.

candidates.name: Miguel Angel Gabela
candidates.notes: Miami City Commission District 1.

candidates.name: Damian Pardo
candidates.notes: Miami City Commission District 2.

candidates.name: Rolando Escalona
candidates.notes: Miami City Commission District 3.

candidates.name: Ralph Rafael Rosado
candidates.notes: Miami City Commission District 4.

candidates.name: Christine King
candidates.notes: Miami City Commission District 5.

candidates.name: Daniella Levine Cava
candidates.notes: First woman elected Miami-Dade County Mayor (2020). Re-elected 2024. Democrat. Proposed FY 2025-26 budget.

candidates.name: Katherine Fernandez Rundle
candidates.notes: Longest-serving State Attorney in Florida. Re-elected unopposed to 7th term (2024). In office since 1993. Over 32 years as Miami-Dade's top prosecutor.

candidates.name: Jane Castor
candidates.notes: In office since 2019. Re-elected 2023 with over 80% of vote. Former Tampa police chief. First openly LGBTQ mayor of Tampa. Was Republican until 2015, switched to Democrat. Nonpartisan office. Tampa Mayor since 2019, re-elected 2023. Former Tampa Police Chief (2009-2015). First openly lesbian mayor of Tampa. Focus on transit expansion, affordable housing, and MacDill AFB relations. Moderate-to-progressive Democrat. Planned Parenthood aligned.

candidates.name: Alan Clendenin
candidates.notes: Tampa City Council District 1. Former Florida Democratic Party vice chair.

candidates.name: Guido Maniscalco
candidates.notes: Tampa City Council District 2.

candidates.name: Lynn Hurtak
candidates.notes: Tampa City Council District 3.

candidates.name: Bill Carlson
candidates.notes: Tampa City Council District 4.

candidates.name: Naya Young
candidates.notes: Tampa City Council District 5.

candidates.name: Charlie Miranda
candidates.notes: Tampa City Council District 6. Long-serving council member.

candidates.name: Luis Viera
candidates.notes: Tampa City Council District 7.

candidates.name: Suzy Lopez
candidates.notes: Sworn in Jan 7, 2025 for first full term. Appointed by Gov. DeSantis in 2023 to replace suspended Andrew Warren. Covers Tampa, Temple Terrace, Plant City. Sworn in alongside Pam Bondi and Ashley Moody.

candidates.name: Donna Deegan
candidates.notes: First woman elected mayor of Jacksonville (2023). Former news anchor. Democrat in a city with majority-Republican council (14R-5D). First Democratic mayor of Jacksonville in 30 years, elected May 2023. Former WTLV news anchor, breast cancer survivor and activist (Donna Foundation). Focus on river conservation, downtown revitalization, and public health. Progressive Democrat. Planned Parenthood endorsed.

candidates.name: Kevin Carrico
candidates.notes: Elected City Council President for 2025-26. Republican.

candidates.name: Nick Howland
candidates.notes: City Council Vice President 2025-26. U.S. Navy veteran. Executive director of The Fire Watch. Republican. Likely next council president (July 2026).

candidates.name: Randy White
candidates.notes: Former City Council President. Republican.

candidates.name: Rory Diamond
candidates.notes: Republican. Active in council proceedings.

candidates.name: Terrance Freeman
candidates.notes: Republican council member.

candidates.name: Matt Carlucci
candidates.notes: Republican. Among wealthiest council members.

candidates.name: Melissa Nelson
candidates.notes: State Attorney covering Duval, Clay, and Nassau counties. Re-elected unopposed 2025. Leads 300+ attorneys and staff. First elected 2016 defeating Angela Corey.

candidates.name: Buddy Dyer
candidates.notes: Longest-serving mayor in Orlando history, in office since 2003. Democrat. Former state senator. Nonpartisan office. Orlando Mayor since 2003, longest-serving mayor in Orlando history. Re-elected 2023. Former Florida State Senator. Focus on I-4 Ultimate, Creative Village development, transit, and tourism economy. Progressive Democrat. Planned Parenthood aligned, pro-LGBTQ, pro-gun-reform.

candidates.name: Tom Keen
candidates.notes: Elected 2025 defeating incumbent Jim Gray. Orlando City Commission District 1.

candidates.name: Tony Ortiz
candidates.notes: Orlando City Commission District 2. Ran unopposed in 2025.

candidates.name: Roger Chapin
candidates.notes: Elected in 2025 runoff for District 3.

candidates.name: Patty Sheehan
candidates.notes: Orlando City Commission District 4. Long-serving commissioner. First openly gay elected official in Central Florida.

candidates.name: Shaniqua Shan Rose
candidates.notes: Re-elected 2025 with ~52% of vote. Orlando City Commission District 5.

candidates.name: Samuel B. Ings
candidates.notes: Re-elected 2025 defeating four challengers. Orlando City Commission District 6. Covers Downtown Orlando and Parramore.

candidates.name: Monique Worrell
candidates.notes: Covers Orange and Osceola counties. Suspended by Gov. DeSantis in 2023 but won re-election in 2024 with ~60%. Sworn back in Jan 2025. Progressive prosecutor. Reduced non-arrest case backlog by 33%.

candidates.name: Andre Dickens
candidates.notes: 61st Mayor of Atlanta. Re-elected Nov 2025 with over 50% outright. Sworn in for 2nd term Jan 5, 2026. Term ends Jan 2030. Has left X/Twitter for Bluesky.

candidates.name: Carlos G. Muñiz
candidates.notes: Chief Justice since July 2024. Appointed to bench by Gov. DeSantis in January 2019. Former General Counsel to Gov. Rick Scott, former Deputy AG of Florida, and former Chief of Staff to AG Pam Bondi. Authored opinion overturning Roe-era abortion precedent in Florida in 2024 (Planned Parenthood v. Moody).

profile.education: University of Virginia (BA), Yale Law School (JD)
profile.background: Appointed Jan 2019 by Gov. DeSantis. Former Deputy AG of Florida.

candidates.name: Charles T. Canady
candidates.notes: Appointed to Florida Supreme Court in 2008 by Gov. Charlie Crist. Served as Chief Justice 2010-2012 and 2018-2022. Former US Representative (R-FL) 1993-2001. Known for originalist jurisprudence. Authored the 2022 opinion ending the Florida Supreme Court's 30-year practice of issuing advisory opinions on redistricting.

profile.background: US House 1993-2001. Appointed to FL Supreme Court 2008 by Gov. Crist.

candidates.name: Jorge Labarga
candidates.notes: Appointed to Florida Supreme Court 2009 by Gov. Charlie Crist. Served as Chief Justice 2014-2018. Most moderate member of the court. First Cuban-American to serve as Chief Justice of the Florida Supreme Court. Frequent dissenter in the post-DeSantis conservative majority era.

profile.background: Appointed 2009 by Gov. Crist. Served as Chief Justice 2014-2018.

candidates.name: John D. Couriel
candidates.notes: Appointed to Florida Supreme Court in May 2020 by Gov. DeSantis. Former federal prosecutor in Miami. Conservative-originalist jurist.

profile.background: Appointed May 2020 by Gov. DeSantis. Former federal prosecutor.

candidates.name: Jamie R. Grosshans
candidates.notes: Appointed to Florida Supreme Court September 2020 by Gov. DeSantis. Former Fifth District Court of Appeal judge. Known for textualist/originalist interpretation.

profile.background: Appointed September 2020 by Gov. DeSantis.

candidates.name: Renatha Francis
candidates.notes: Appointed to Florida Supreme Court in 2022 by Gov. DeSantis, making her the first Jamaican-born justice on the Florida Supreme Court. Conservative jurist.

profile.background: Appointed 2022 by Gov. DeSantis. First Jamaican-born FL Supreme Court justice.

candidates.name: Meredith L. Sasso
candidates.notes: Appointed to Florida Supreme Court in 2023 by Gov. DeSantis. Previously served on the Sixth District Court of Appeal. Conservative-originalist.

profile.background: Appointed 2023 by Gov. DeSantis.

candidates.name: John E. Dailey
candidates.notes: Mayor of Tallahassee since 2018, re-elected 2022. Former Leon County Commissioner. Has supported Tallahassee's sanctuary-adjacent policies and progressive urban initiatives. Tallahassee Mayor since 2018, re-elected 2022. Has supported progressive urban policy: affordable housing bonds, police reform measures, climate action plan. Leon County is a Democratic stronghold. Planned Parenthood aligned. Not a strong "sanctuary city" designation but declined to expand ICE cooperation.

profile.background: Leon County Commissioner 2006-2018. Mayor since 2018.

candidates.name: Dianne Williams-Cox
candidates.notes: Tallahassee City Commissioner since 2018. Re-elected 2022. Tallahassee City Commissioner since 2018, re-elected 2022. Former executive director of Capital City Youth Services. Progressive Democrat. Focus on affordable housing, youth services, community safety.

candidates.name: Curtis Richardson
candidates.notes: Tallahassee City Commissioner since 2014. Former Florida State Representative. Tallahassee City Commissioner since 2014. Former Florida State Representative (2000-2008). Progressive Democrat. Focus on economic development, neighborhood revitalization.

candidates.name: Jeremy Matlow
candidates.notes: Tallahassee City Commissioner since 2018. Progressive activist wing. Tallahassee City Commissioner since 2018. Progressive/activist wing of the Democratic Party. Pizza restaurateur (Gaines Street Pies). Focus on affordable housing, police accountability, and ethics reform.

candidates.name: Jacqueline "Jack" Porter
candidates.notes: Tallahassee City Commissioner since 2020. Progressive-wing Democrat. Tallahassee City Commissioner since 2020. Progressive Democrat, aligned with Matlow on reform-oriented policies. Former Leon County Commission staffer.

candidates.name: Kenneth T. Welch
candidates.notes: First Black mayor of St. Petersburg, elected November 2021. Previously served as Pinellas County Commissioner from 2000-2020. Focus on affordable housing and Historic Gas Plant / Tropicana Field redevelopment. St. Petersburg's first Black mayor, elected November 2021. Focus on Historic Gas Plant / Tropicana Field district redevelopment, affordable housing, environmental resilience. Pinellas County Commissioner 2000-2020. Progressive Democrat. Member of Mayor Innovation Project.

profile.background: Pinellas County Commissioner 2000-2020. Mayor since 2022.

candidates.name: Copley Gerdes
candidates.notes: St. Petersburg City Council, District 1. Elected 2021. St. Petersburg City Council, District 1. Elected 2021. Moderate Democrat, focused on business district growth and waterfront development.

candidates.name: Brandi Gabbard
candidates.notes: St. Petersburg City Council, District 2, since 2017. St. Petersburg City Council, District 2 since 2017, re-elected 2021. Democrat. Real estate professional. Focus on housing policy.

candidates.name: Mike Harting
candidates.notes: St. Petersburg City Council, District 3. Elected 2023.

candidates.name: Lisset Hanewicz
candidates.notes: St. Petersburg City Council, District 4. Elected 2021.

candidates.name: Deborah Figgs-Sanders
candidates.notes: St. Petersburg City Council, District 5. Elected 2019, re-elected 2023. St. Petersburg City Council, District 5 since 2019, re-elected 2023. Democrat. Former educator. Focus on South St. Petersburg economic development.

candidates.name: Gina Driscoll
candidates.notes: St. Petersburg City Council, District 6. Elected 2017, re-elected 2021. St. Petersburg City Council, District 6 since 2017, re-elected 2021. Democrat. Former downtown business owner. Focus on downtown growth.

candidates.name: Corey Givens Jr.
candidates.notes: St. Petersburg City Council, District 7. Elected 2025 special election.

candidates.name: Richie Floyd
candidates.notes: St. Petersburg City Council, District 8, since 2021. Democratic Socialist. St. Petersburg City Council, District 8 since 2021. Democratic Socialists of America member. Former teacher. Strong progressive on housing, transit, and labor issues.

candidates.name: Dean Trantalis
candidates.notes: Mayor of Fort Lauderdale since 2018, re-elected unopposed in 2022. Former Fort Lauderdale City Commissioner. First openly gay mayor of Fort Lauderdale. Supported Pride programming, affordable housing, and climate resilience initiatives. Fort Lauderdale Mayor since 2018, re-elected unopposed 2022. First openly gay mayor of Fort Lauderdale. Supported climate resilience measures, Stonewall Pride programming, affordable housing initiatives. Coalition of Mayors for Gun Safety member. Progressive Democrat.

profile.background: Fort Lauderdale City Commissioner 2003-2006 and 2013-2018. Mayor since 2018.

candidates.name: Esteban Bovo Jr.
candidates.notes: Mayor of Hialeah since November 2021. Cuban-American Republican. Former Miami-Dade County Commissioner (2011-2020) and Florida State Representative (2008-2010). Strong conservative on immigration enforcement, anti-communism (Cuba), and fiscal policy. Hialeah Mayor since November 2021. Heavily Cuban-American city that votes strongly Republican. Hard line on immigration enforcement and anti-communist foreign policy (esp. Cuba, Venezuela, Nicaragua). Supported DeSantis-era policies including parental rights. Former FL State Rep (2008-2010) and Miami-Dade County Commissioner (2011-2020).

profile.education: Miami Dade College (AA), Florida International University (BA)
profile.background: FL House 2008-2010. Miami-Dade Commissioner 2011-2020. Hialeah Mayor since 2021.

candidates.name: John Herbst
candidates.notes: Fort Lauderdale City Commissioner, District 1. Former Fort Lauderdale City Auditor for 17 years. Fiscal conservative on a Democratic-leaning commission.

candidates.name: Steven Glassman
candidates.notes: Fort Lauderdale City Commissioner, District 2. Architect and historic preservation advocate. Progressive Democrat aligned with Mayor Trantalis.

candidates.name: Pamela Beasley-Pittman
candidates.notes: Fort Lauderdale City Commissioner, District 3, first elected 2022. Community advocate. Progressive Democrat.

candidates.name: Warren Sturman
candidates.notes: Fort Lauderdale City Commissioner, District 4, elected 2024. Physician. Progressive Democrat.

candidates.name: Gregory Tony
candidates.notes: Broward County Sheriff since 2019. Appointed by Gov. DeSantis in January 2019 after Scott Israel was suspended over the Parkland shooting response. Subsequently elected 2020 and re-elected 2024. First Black sheriff of Broward County. Background as former Coral Springs police officer. Registered Democrat; has clashed with progressive reform advocates over use-of-force policies.

profile.education: Florida Atlantic University (BA), University of South Florida (MA)
profile.background: Coral Springs police officer 2005-2016. BSO Sheriff since 2019.

candidates.name: Rosie Cordero-Stutz
candidates.notes: First elected Miami-Dade County Sheriff since 1966 (sworn in January 2025). Won November 2024 election against James Reyes. Cuban-American. 30+ year veteran of Miami-Dade Police Department, rose to Assistant Director. Law-and-order Republican. Strong on anti-human-trafficking, immigration enforcement coordination with ICE, and cartel interdiction.

profile.background: 30+ year Miami-Dade police career, rose to Assistant Director. Sworn in January 2025 as first elected MDC Sheriff.

candidates.name: Wayne Ivey
candidates.notes: Brevard County Sheriff since January 2013, re-elected 2016, 2020, and 2024. Nationally known for the weekly "Wheel of Fugitive" video series. 35+ year law enforcement career including Florida Department of Law Enforcement Special Agent in Charge. Strong law-and-order conservative. Vocal on Second Amendment, school safety (Sheriff's Guardian Program — armed school personnel), immigration enforcement cooperation with ICE, and anti-drug policy. Outspoken on the Parkland shooting and police legitimacy. NRA A+ rating. Member of Constitutional Sheriffs and Peace Officers Association.

profile.background: FDLE Special Agent in Charge (Brevard/Indian River). Brevard County Sheriff since 2013.

candidates.name: Paul Alfrey
candidates.notes: Mayor of Melbourne, FL since November 2020, re-elected November 2024. Republican-aligned in Melbourne's nonpartisan mayor-council system. Previously served on Melbourne City Council. Focus on Space Coast economic development, public safety, infrastructure, and fiscal responsibility. Supportive of local law enforcement and Brevard County Sheriff's Office cooperation. Business background in construction.

profile.background: Construction industry. Former Melbourne City Council. Mayor since 2020.

candidates.name: Thad Altman
candidates.notes: Brevard County Commissioner District 5 since January 2025. Republican. Former Florida State Senator (2008-2016) and State Representative (2016-2022). Long career in Brevard County politics focused on Space Coast issues (KSC/Cape Canaveral), Indian River Lagoon environmental protection, and fiscal policy. NRA A rating as legislator. Heritage Action moderate-to-conservative. Pro-life.

profile.education: Florida Institute of Technology (BS, Business Administration)
profile.background: FL House 1982-1988 and 2016-2022. FL Senate 2008-2016. Brevard County Commissioner since Jan 2025.

candidates.name: Tim Bobanic
candidates.notes: Brevard County Supervisor of Elections since January 2024 (appointed by Gov. DeSantis to fill Lori Scott's vacancy), elected November 2024 to a full term. Previously served 20+ years in Brevard elections administration, including as Deputy Supervisor. Focus on election integrity, voter-roll maintenance, and secure ballot infrastructure. Republican.

profile.background: 20+ years in Brevard elections office. Appointed Supervisor January 2024, elected November 2024.

candidates.name: Rachel Sadoff
candidates.notes: Brevard County Clerk of the Courts. Directly elected constitutional officer responsible for court records, official county records, real estate recordings, county finances, and minutes of the Board of County Commissioners. Longtime Brevard clerk office career.

profile.background: Brevard County Clerk of the Courts.

candidates.name: Lisa Cullen
candidates.notes: Brevard County Tax Collector, CFC (Certified Florida Collector). Directly elected constitutional officer; the tax collector administers property tax collection, driver license and tag services, business tax receipts, and hunting/fishing licenses for the county.

profile.background: Brevard County Tax Collector (CFC).

candidates.name: Dana Blickley
candidates.notes: Brevard County Property Appraiser, CFA (Certified Florida Appraiser). Directly elected constitutional officer responsible for determining the just value of all real and tangible personal property in the county, administering homestead and other property tax exemptions, and maintaining property ownership records. In office since 2013.

profile.background: Brevard County Property Appraiser since 2013.
